# OkygraphView API Design - Final

## Core Principle: Overloaded `write()` Methods

The base class uses **method overloading** to handle escaping automatically based on the type of the argument.

## Write Methods

### 1. `write(String text)` - Auto-escapes
```java
protected void write(String text) throws IOException {
    w.write(escape(text));
}
```

**Usage:** Expressions that return strings
```java
write(user.getName());        // Escaped: &lt;script&gt;...
write(product.getTitle());    // Escaped
```

**Generated by transpiler for:** `{user.getName()}`

### 2. `write(int/long/double/boolean)` - No escaping
```java
protected void write(int value) throws IOException {
    w.write(String.valueOf(value));
}
// ... and overloads for long, double, boolean
```

**Usage:** Numeric and boolean expressions
```java
write(user.getAge());         // 42
write(product.getPrice());    // 19.99
write(user.isActive());       // true
```

**Generated by transpiler for:** `{user.age}`, `{product.price}`, `{user.active}`

### 3. `write(char c)` - Escapes
```java
protected void write(char c) throws IOException {
    w.write(escape(String.valueOf(c)));
}
```

**Usage:** Single character expressions
```java
write('<');  // &lt;
write('A');  // A
```

### 4. `write(Raw raw)` - No escaping (DANGEROUS)
```java
protected void write(Raw raw) throws IOException {
    if (raw != null && raw.content != null) {
        w.write(raw.content);
    }
}
```

**Usage:** Trusted HTML content
```java
write(raw("<strong>Bold</strong>"));  // <strong>Bold</strong>
write(raw(trustedHtmlFromDb));        // Direct output
```

**Generated by transpiler for:** `{raw(htmlContent)}`

### 5. `writeRaw(String literal)` - Package-private
```java
void writeRaw(String text) throws IOException {
    if (text != null) {
        w.write(text);
    }
}
```

**Usage:** HTML literals in templates (transpiler only)
```java
writeRaw("<div class=\"container\">");
writeRaw("\n");
```

**Generated by transpiler for:** HTML text between expressions

## Helper Methods

### `raw(String html)` - Returns Raw wrapper
```java
protected static class Raw {
    final String content;
    Raw(String content) { this.content = content; }
}

protected Raw raw(String html) {
    return new Raw(html);
}
```

**User calls:** `{raw(trustedHtml)}`
**Transpiler generates:** `write(raw(trustedHtml))`
**Result:** No escaping

### `jsEscape(String text)` - JavaScript string escaping
```java
protected String jsEscape(String text) {
    // Escapes: \, ", ', \n, \r, \t, \b, \f
    return escaped;
}
```

**User calls:** `{jsEscape(message)}`
**Transpiler generates:** `writeRaw(jsEscape(message))`
**Result:** JavaScript-safe string (no double-escaping)

### `urlEncode(String text)` - URL encoding
```java
protected String urlEncode(String text) {
    return URLEncoder.encode(text, StandardCharsets.UTF_8);
}
```

**User calls:** `{urlEncode(searchTerm)}`
**Transpiler generates:** `writeRaw(urlEncode(searchTerm))`
**Result:** URL-encoded string (no double-escaping)

## Transpiler Generation Rules

| Template Input | Java Expression Type | Generated Code |
|----------------|---------------------|----------------|
| `{user.name}` | `String` | `write(user.name)` |
| `{user.age}` | `int` | `write(user.age)` |
| `{product.price}` | `double` | `write(product.price)` |
| `{user.active}` | `boolean` | `write(user.active)` |
| `{raw(html)}` | `Raw` | `write(raw(html))` |
| `{jsEscape(text)}` | `String` | `writeRaw(jsEscape(text))` |
| `{urlEncode(text)}` | `String` | `writeRaw(urlEncode(text))` |
| `<div>` | HTML literal | `writeRaw("<div>")` |

**Special case:** Helper methods (`jsEscape`, `urlEncode`) return already-processed strings, so transpiler uses `writeRaw()` to avoid double-escaping.

## Complete Example

### Template
```html
<div class="user">
    <h1>{user.name}</h1>
    <p>Age: {user.age}</p>
    <p>Email: {user.email}</p>
    <div>{raw(user.bio)}</div>
    <script>
        var name = "{jsEscape(user.name)}";
    </script>
</div>
```

### Generated Code
```java
@Override
protected void render() throws IOException {
    writeRaw("<div class=\"user\">");
    writeRaw("\n    <h1>");
    write(user.name);  // Escaped
    writeRaw("</h1>");
    writeRaw("\n    <p>Age: ");
    write(user.age);   // No escape needed
    writeRaw("</p>");
    writeRaw("\n    <p>Email: ");
    write(user.email); // Escaped
    writeRaw("</p>");
    writeRaw("\n    <div>");
    write(raw(user.bio)); // Raw, no escape
    writeRaw("</div>");
    writeRaw("\n    <script>");
    writeRaw("\n        var name = \"");
    writeRaw(jsEscape(user.name)); // JS escaped, no double-escape
    writeRaw("\";\n    </script>");
    writeRaw("\n</div>");
}
```

## Benefits of This Design

### 1. Type Safety
The compiler ensures correct escaping based on type:
```java
write("text");           // Escaped
write(42);               // Not escaped
write(raw("html"));      // Not escaped (intentional)
```

### 2. Performance
- No runtime type checking (`instanceof`)
- Direct method dispatch
- Optimal for each type

### 3. User Experience
- **Simple:** Just call `write(expr)`
- **Safe:** Strings are escaped by default
- **Flexible:** Override `escape()` for custom types
- **Explicit:** Use `raw()` when you want no escaping

### 4. Transpiler Simplicity
The transpiler just needs to:
1. Detect expression type (string, int, method call, etc.)
2. Generate `write(expr)` or `writeRaw(expr)`
3. No complex escaping logic in transpiler

## Custom Escaping

Users can override `escape()` for custom types:

```java
public class MyView extends OkygraphView {
    @Override
    protected String escape(Object value) {
        if (value instanceof SafeHtml) {
            return value.toString(); // Already safe
        }
        if (value instanceof Markdown) {
            return markdownToHtml(value).toString();
        }
        return super.escape(value); // Default HTML escape
    }

    @Override
    protected void render() throws IOException {
        write(safeHtmlObject);    // No escape
        write(markdownObject);    // Converted
        write(regularString);     // HTML escaped
    }
}
```

## Transpiler Implementation Notes

### Expression Type Detection

The transpiler needs to determine the type of each expression:

1. **Literal types:** `42`, `3.14`, `true`, `"string"`
2. **Method calls:**
   - Check return type: `int`, `String`, etc.
   - Special methods: `raw()`, `jsEscape()`, `urlEncode()`
3. **Field access:** Check field type
4. **Complex expressions:** May need type inference or default to `String`

### Special Method Handling

```java
// Template: {raw(html)}
// Detection: Method name is "raw"
// Generate: write(raw(html))

// Template: {jsEscape(text)}
// Detection: Method name is "jsEscape" or "urlEncode"
// Generate: writeRaw(jsEscape(text))  // Avoid double-escape
```

### Default Behavior

If type is unknown or complex:
- **Assume `Object`**
- **Convert to String:** `String.valueOf(expr)`
- **Escape:** `write(String.valueOf(expr))`

This ensures safety by default.

## Testing Coverage

✅ **39 tests in OkygraphViewTest:**
- Basic rendering (string, writer, output stream)
- HTML escaping
- XSS prevention
- Raw HTML bypass
- JavaScript escaping (no double-escape)
- URL encoding (no double-escape)
- Try/catch buffering
- Custom escape override
- Primitive types (int, long, double, boolean, char)

## Summary

The overloaded `write()` design:
1. ✅ **Safe by default** - Strings are escaped
2. ✅ **Efficient** - No runtime type checks
3. ✅ **Simple** - Just `write(expr)`
4. ✅ **Flexible** - Override `escape()` for custom types
5. ✅ **Type-safe** - Compiler enforces correct usage
6. ✅ **Transpiler-friendly** - Simple code generation rules
